seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service ai email assistant

content.intro: The Azure AI Agent Service enables building sophisticated AI Email Assistants that process incoming emails, detect intent, generate draft responses, handle attachments, and route for approval. With enterprise-grade Azure OpenAI integration and grounding capabilities, it streamlines email workflows but introduces risks like PII leakage, inappropriate tone, and policy violations. Vex guardrails ensure reliable, secure operation by validating outputs against enterprise policies before actions like sending emails.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant ingests emails via Azure Event Grid or Logic Apps, triggering an Azure AI Agent thread. The agent uses Azure OpenAI for intent detection (e.g., 'respond', 'forward', 'archive'), then invokes tools for drafting responses, searching attachments via Azure AI Search, or grounding with Bing Search. Generated drafts pass through approval workflows with human-in-the-loop via Azure AI Studio before final send via Microsoft Graph API.

Key components include agent threads for conversation state, tool calling for email actions (send, attach, search), and grounding data sources for accuracy. Enterprise security controls enforce RBAC and DLP, but lack runtime validation for tone, PII, or policy compliance.

Vex integrates as middleware in the agent execution pipeline, wrapping tool calls and final outputs with policy checks.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Email Drafts
risks.description: Agent generates responses containing unfiltered PII from email threads or attachments
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection policy scans all outputs before send approval

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Detection Failure
risks.description: Sarcastic, aggressive, or unprofessional language slips through to executives
risks.mitigation: Vex sentiment and tone analysis with enterprise style guide enforcement

risks.name: Unauthorized Recipient Addition
risks.description: Agent suggests CC/BCC to unapproved external domains violating DLP policy
risks.mitigation: Vex recipient validation against corporate allow/block lists

risks.name: Outdated Grounding Data
risks.description: Bing Search returns stale policy info causing compliance violations in responses
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness validation and enterprise knowledge base prioritization

implementation.title: Email Intent Detection Agent
implementation.description: Create Azure AI Agent thread for classifying email intent and routing to appropriate tools
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentThread

thread = AgentThread.create(
    project_name="email-assistant",
    instructions="You are an email processing agent. Classify intent: respond|forward|archive|delegate"
)

thread.add_message(content=inbound_email.body)
result = thread.run()

intents = ['respond', 'forward', 'archive', 'delegate']
intent = next((i for i in intents if i in result.content.lower()), 'archive')

print(f'Detected intent: {intent}')
